name: REST API endpoint soak — all routes, 50 concurrent workers, 5 minutes
aim: All 11 endpoints must respond within p99<50ms with zero unexpected status codes for 5 minutes under 50 concurrent workers

threshold.rationale: Dashboard polls these endpoints for operator actions — p99>50ms causes visible lag

on_exceed: Dashboard operator actions (policy update, simulation control, alert ack) experience visible latency or errors — control room responsiveness degraded

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 11 endpoints with 50 concurrent workers for 5 minutes via httptest.Server backed by real api.NewServer
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: p99<50ms: operators expect sub-100ms response from control actions; 50ms leaves headroom for network
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard buttons feel sluggish or timeout — operator cannot respond quickly to incidents
answered_questions.how_load_was_generated: 50 goroutines round-robining across all endpoints; real http.Client with 5s timeout
answered_questions.how_metrics_were_measured: Wall-clock time per request measured by caller; p50/p95/p99/p100 computed from sorted slice

name: Server correctness under 10k requests per endpoint
aim: 10000 requests per endpoint × 10 endpoints: correct status codes and JSON response bodies throughout

threshold.rationale: Every request must return the exact documented response

on_exceed: Server returns wrong status codes or invalid JSON under load — dashboard receives malformed responses

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Zero tolerance — every request must return exactly the documented response shape
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard JavaScript fails to parse response → UI shows error state
answered_questions.how_metrics_were_measured: HTTP status code + JSON field check per response

name: Hub.GetLastPayload: 100 readers + 10Hz broadcast for 60s
aim: 100 concurrent GetLastPayload callers while Broadcast at 10Hz: p99 < 1ms

threshold.rationale: /api/v1/snapshot calls GetLastPayload — must be fast for dashboard first-load

on_exceed: GetLastPayload blocks → /snapshot HTTP responses pile up → dashboard first-load timeout

answered_questions.what_was_tested: 100 goroutines tight-looping GetLastPayload for 60s while 1 broadcaster at 10Hz
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 1ms p99: RLock on a struct field is sub-microsecond; 1ms is extremely generous
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: REST /snapshot endpoint latency > 1ms → dashboard HTTP timeout on initial load
answered_questions.how_metrics_were_measured: time.Since(t0) per GetLastPayload call; p99 from sorted slice

name: Hub max client cap enforced at connection time
aim: With maxClients=5, the 6th connection must be rejected (HTTP 503)

threshold.rationale: Unbounded client acceptance leads to OOM under connection flood

on_exceed: Hub accepts unlimited WebSocket connections → attacker or misbehaving dashboard opens thousands of connections → OOM kill

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Hard cap prevents resource exhaustion under connection flood
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Each reconnecting dashboard tab creates a new connection → OOM kill
answered_questions.how_load_was_generated: Sequential connection attempts via dialWebSocket helper
answered_questions.how_metrics_were_measured: dialWebSocket returns error if server sends 503 instead of 101
